_notes: BPE tokenizer task. Two structural risks the rubric must navigate: (1) BPE is one of the most-published algorithms on the public internet — every capable model has seen many reference implementations verbatim, so memorization can pass behavioral gates without real work; (2) instruction.md is a detailed prompt-as-spec dictating file path, function names, signatures, constants, section structure, and comment density — positive scoring of dictated structure would conflate obedience-to-prompt with capability. v3 rubric scores the behavioral contract only, deliberately accepts multiple valid output formats, never references oracle-specific identifiers (e.g. the oracle's test_strings list or print-format literals), and front-loads an anti-stub goal that credits real iterative pair-counting/merging.

user_asks_indexed.text: Implement byte-pair encoding: count adjacent pairs, greedily merge the most frequent pair, record merge rules in priority order
Round-trip tests: 6 test strings ... each shows [PASS/FAIL] / All round-trip tests print [PASS]
Compression ratio ... Must be >= 1.5x / Compression ratio >= 1.5x
Print progress every 32 steps: merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length
No imports outside stdlib / zero dependencies / os, urllib.request, collections.Counter (all stdlib)
7 named functions with specified signatures: load_data, get_pair_counts, apply_merge, train_bpe, build_vocab, encode, decode

user_asks_not_scored.reason: 180-200 line target is a comment-density proxy, not behavior; scoring it would penalize correct solutions that are shorter or longer. The P2P anti-stub floor (>=80) is the only line-count signal we need.
Cosmetic. Scoring comment ratios would credit decoration over correctness.

user_asks_not_scored.source: instruction.md L91-99 (Section 6 output blocks) + L122 (=== headers)
user_asks_not_scored.reason: Print-format literals (e.g. the oracle's 'ratio: 2.01x' vs other valid 'Compression: 2.01x') are stylistic; the verifier's narrow regex is a known false-negative source we offset here with format-tolerant goals.
Constant value is a hyperparameter pick; any value that yields the asked-for behavioral outcomes (>=1.5x compression, real training trace) satisfies the underlying intent.
Convention statement, not a behavioral contract — BPE itself is deterministic without it.

oracle_identifiers_explicitly_avoided: test_strings list contents (Emma/Xiomara/Mary-Jane/O'Brien/Z) — oracle-chosen; goal_2 phrases the diversity requirement abstractly
exact === section header literals — oracle-chosen presentation, not user-dictated
'merge N/M:' specific separator format — oracle-chosen presentation; user only dictated the fields, not the exact separator
'ratio: <float>x' specific format — oracle-chosen; instruction L95 says 'Compression ratio ... Must be >= 1.5x' without dictating the literal output format
227-line oracle length / oracle's exact function bodies
Top-20-merges block + Tokenization-example block exact rendering from oracle Section 6 — user asked for the blocks (instruction L97-99) but the exact format is oracle-chosen
Example word 'Elizabeth' or 'Jennifer' — oracle demo choices, not part of the contract
user_dictated_outputs_explicitly_pinned: [PASS/FAIL] bracketed marker for round-trip tests — instruction L93 verbatim: 'each shows [PASS/FAIL]' and L135: 'All round-trip tests print [PASS]'. Scored in goal_2.

Progress field set 'merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length' for training progress lines — instruction L69 verbatim: 'Print progress every 32 steps: merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length'. Scored in goal_4.
7 function names from Functions Summary table (load_data, get_pair_counts, apply_merge, train_bpe, build_vocab, encode, decode) — instruction L107-114 user-dictated. Scored in goal_8.

completeness_goals.goal: Do not stub, fake, or hard-code the BPE behavior. The submitted script must perform genuine iterative pair-frequency counting and greedy merging over a real text corpus — distinct merge events must reflect the corpus's actual pair statistics, the sequence length must shrink across training, and round-trip results must be produced by a working encode/decode round trip rather than a precomputed mapping. Any evidence that the round-trip / compression / progress signals were produced by literal constants, hard-coded mappings, or skipped training loops zeroes this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort guardrail. Justified by structural risk: BPE is one of the most-memorized algorithms in pretraining data and the verifier surface (string matching on stdout) is trivially gameable. Without this goal, a model that prints fake '[PASS]' lines, a hard-coded 'ratio: 2.01x', and 8 synthetic 'merge N/M' lines could max out the rubric. Anchored to USER asks U_BPE_ALGO + U_ROUNDTRIP + U_COMPRESSION + U_TRAINING_PROGRESS — not an oracle-only constraint; the user explicitly described the algorithm to be implemented.

completeness_goals.goal: Deliver a self-contained Python script at 01-foundations/microtokenizer.py (the path the user named in the spec) with valid Python syntax that executes to completion without raising an unhandled exception and within a reasonable runtime budget (a couple of minutes on a small corpus). Any reasonable code organization is acceptable for THIS goal — one large function, several short functions, or a class-based decomposition all satisfy this script-executes contract; the user's specific function names are scored separately in goal_8.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline deliverable. Combines U_FILE_PATH + U_RUNS_CLEAN into a single behavioral contract: a working script at the named location. Deliberately decoupled from the oracle's specific function decomposition and the spec's 180-200 line cosmetic target. The 'reasonable runtime' phrasing tracks U_RUNS_CLEAN's <2 min budget without binding to the oracle's 12s.

completeness_goals.goal: Implement encode and decode (under any name or as part of any structure) such that decode(encode(text)) == text holds for a diverse set of inputs covering at least: a common short alphabetic string, a string with internal punctuation or a hyphen, a string with an apostrophe, an empty string, and a single-character string (5+ distinct categories, 6+ total cases). All reported cases must pass. The user explicitly dictated the per-case status format in instruction.md (L93 'each shows [PASS/FAIL]' and L135 'All round-trip tests print [PASS]'): the script MUST report per-case status using the literal bracketed tokens '[PASS]' (and '[FAIL]' on failure) — the brackets are part of the user-named format. Alternative renderings such as bare 'PASS'/'FAIL' labels, checkmarks, X marks, emoji, or coloured-only output do NOT satisfy this goal; they were never named by the user. Test-input contents are described by category (not by oracle-specific strings) to avoid oracle-memorization advantage, but the output format token IS pinned because the user pinned it.
completeness_goals.rationale: Fundamental correctness property of BPE — round-trip identity is the ground-truth behavioral test for tokenization. Tracks U_ROUNDTRIP. Per the structured_output_pinned_when_user_did bar, '[PASS/FAIL]' is a USER-dictated literal (instruction.md L93,L135) and must be scored as written, not generalized to 'checkmarks or equivalents'. Previously phrased format-tolerantly under the assumption '[PASS]' was an oracle quirk; gemini-3.1-pro-preview audit (2026-06-06) flagged this as over-correction — narrow-test critique from SWE-bench Verified applies to ORACLE identifiers, not USER-dictated output format. Test-input contents (the test_strings list) remain described by category to preserve the anti-memorization guarantee on a separate axis.

completeness_goals.goal: Train BPE merge rules on a real natural-language text corpus and achieve a compression ratio of at least 1.5x (original byte count divided by final token count >= 1.5). The script must report this ratio in clearly parseable output: any labeled numeric form is acceptable (e.g. 'ratio: 2.01x', 'Compression: 2.01x', 'compression_ratio = 2.01', 'BPE compression 2.0x', etc.) — the exact literal is not scored.
completeness_goals.rationale: Proves the training loop discovers compressible structure rather than just running. Tracks U_COMPRESSION. Format intentionally broad to avoid the verifier's narrow 'ratio:\s*<float>x' literal, which the canonical audit flagged as penalizing perfectly correct alternatives.

completeness_goals.goal: Demonstrate that the training loop ran a meaningful number of merge iterations by emitting periodic progress lines during training. The user explicitly enumerated the required progress fields in instruction.md L69 verbatim: 'Print progress every 32 steps: merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length'. Each periodic progress line MUST include all four user-named fields: (1) merge index, (2) pair (the pair being merged), (3) frequency (count of that pair), and (4) sequence length (current token sequence length after merge). Any reasonable line-formatting/separator choice is acceptable (e.g. 'merge 32/256 pair=(101,32) freq=4123 seqlen=98412' or comma/colon/tab variants), but a progress line missing one or more of the four user-named fields does NOT satisfy this goal. A bare tqdm progress bar, or progress lines that only report 'step K/N' without the pair/frequency/seqlen fields, fail this goal. Cadence: at least 8 such fully-fielded progress lines during training (instruction L69 says every 32 steps over 256 merges => 8 lines).
completeness_goals.rationale: Tracks U_TRAINING_PROGRESS. Per the structured_output_pinned_when_user_did bar, the user-named field set 'merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length' is a USER-dictated structured output (instruction.md L69) and must be scored as written. Previously broadened to 'tqdm bar OR final summary OR any progress format' under format-tolerance reasoning; gemini-3.1-pro-preview audit (2026-06-06) flagged this as under-pinning a literal user spec. Per-line separator format remains unscored (the user named the fields, not their layout); the field set itself is now pinned.

completeness_goals.goal: Use only Python standard library modules. No pip-installed or third-party imports may appear in the script (typical stdlib modules such as os, math, random, collections, urllib, itertools, functools, re, typing, pathlib, etc. are all acceptable).
completeness_goals.rationale: Constraint stated by the user. Tracks U_STDLIB. Unambiguous and implementation-agnostic.

completeness_goals.goal: Train on a real natural-language corpus the user pointed at (the makemore names dataset) or an equivalent locally cached copy with comparable scale and statistical structure. Synthetic strings, repeated tokens, or trivially compressible filler chosen to game the ratio do not satisfy this.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming companion to goal_0 and goal_3. Without it, training on 'aaaaa...' would trivially yield high compression. Tracks U_CORPUS. The exact URL and filename are not scored — an offline-equivalent natural-text corpus of similar shape is acceptable, since the user's intent is 'real text', not 'this specific HTTP endpoint'.

completeness_goals.goal: If the agent observes the user's follow-up commit request (a single short message such as 'commit this'), it commits only the work product (the script it just wrote), avoiding sweeping in unrelated pre-existing repo modifications, and reports the commit succeeded. If the agent never reaches the commit phase because earlier goals failed, this goal is not blocked-on but also not awarded.
completeness_goals.rationale: Tracks the user's Turn-2 ask U_COMMIT. Weighted low because: (a) it depends on reaching a working-script state first; (b) the commit phase is a workflow step, not the substantive BPE deliverable; (c) per user_simulation_prompt.md, the user sends 'commit this' ONLY after the agent has demonstrated success — so this goal also indirectly signals end-to-end task success.

completeness_goals.goal: Honor the user's explicit Functions Summary table (instruction.md L107-114), which dictates 7 specific function names and their signatures: load_data(url, filename) -> bytes; get_pair_counts(token_ids) -> Counter; apply_merge(token_ids, pair, new_id) -> list[int]; train_bpe(token_ids, num_merges) -> ordered merge table; build_vocab(merges) -> dict[int, bytes]; encode(text, merges) -> list[int]; decode(token_ids, vocab) -> str. Credit is partial and proportional: a submission that defines at least 5 of the 7 named callables with reasonably matching argument arity earns this goal; defining all 7 with matching signatures earns it fully; defining 0-4 fails it. A single-function or class-based decomposition that ignores the user's explicit table does NOT satisfy this goal, even if behaviorally correct. Exact return-type annotations and parameter names are not scored — only function names and approximate signature shape.
completeness_goals.rationale: The user dictated a 7-row Functions Summary table with explicit names and signatures (instruction.md L107-114). Per the structured_output_pinned_when_user_did bar, when the user pins structured output the rubric must score it. v3 originally placed this in user_asks_not_scored citing the SWE-bench Verified narrow-test critique — but that critique applies to ORACLE-derived identifier scoring, not to USER-dictated identifier scoring. The user explicitly wrote the table in their own prompt, so following it is following the spec, not memorizing the oracle. Weighted moderately (0.07): meaningful enough to reward spec adherence without dominating behavioral correctness.

completeness_goals.goal: SYSTEM-LEVEL ANTI-EFFORT FLOOR (zero-weight gate that zeroes the entire rubric score). The submission must represent genuine implementation work performed in the agent's working tree. If any of the following are observed, the ENTIRE rubric score is set to 0.0 regardless of any other goal's verdict: (a) the agent produced a zero-edit or empty patch (no real file was written at 01-foundations/microtokenizer.py); (b) the agent exfiltrated, copied, or `git checkout`'d a reference implementation from another branch, another repo, or a cached/oracle source rather than authoring it; (c) the script at the target path is a thin wrapper that shells out to, imports from, or fetches from an external location to obtain the BPE behavior; (d) the script's body is a hard-coded transcript of pre-recorded stdout designed to satisfy string-matching gates without performing the algorithm. This is a binary kill-switch, not a scored goal — it carries weight 0.0 and contributes nothing to the positive score, but a confirmed violation zeroes the total.
completeness_goals.rationale: True system-level anti-effort floor distinct from goal_0's task-specific anti-stub (which only zeroes its own 0.18 weight). Per the v3 anti_effort_floor bar: a rubric needs a hard zero on the TOTAL score for cheats like git-ops exfiltration, empty-patch submissions, and stdout fabrication — independent of how well the surface output satisfies behavioral goals. Without this, a hardcoded transcript that prints fake '[PASS]' lines and a fake ratio could still earn up to ~0.82 from goals 1-7. Anchored to U_BPE_ALGO (real algorithm) + U_FILE_PATH (real authored file). Weight 0.0 so it does not perturb the positive-score budget; its only effect is the kill-switch zero on confirmed violations.

_weight_audit.differences_summary: v3 weights are NOT a copy of F2P gate weights. v3 introduces goal_0 (anti-stub, no F2P gate equivalent) at 0.17, goal_7 (commit workflow) at 0.03, goal_8 (user-dictated function names) at 0.07, and goal_floor (system-level anti-effort kill-switch) at weight 0.0 — none have an F2P counterpart. Weights for goals that share a domain with an F2P gate differ in magnitude: goal_2 (roundtrip) 0.20 vs F2P 0.25; goal_3 (compression) 0.16 vs F2P 0.20; goal_4 (training trace) 0.07 vs F2P 0.15; goal_5 (stdlib) 0.07 vs F2P 0.10; goal_1 (script-runs) absorbs both F2P gate_syntax(0.10) and gate_executes(0.20) into a single behavioral goal at 0.18. The rubric reflects human-reviewer credit for partial solutions, not the verifier's reward shape — per the v3 bar.

anti_effort_floor.effect: binary kill-switch zeroing the entire rubric score on confirmed git-ops exfiltration, empty-patch submission, fetched-not-authored implementation, or hard-coded stdout transcript

decomposition_notes: 10 goals total: 1 task-specific anti-stub (goal_0) + 3 cores (goal_1/2/3) + 5 secondaries (goal_4/5/6/7/8) + 1 system-level anti-effort floor (goal_floor at weight 0.0, kill-switch). Positive weights sum to 1.00 across goal_0..goal_8; goal_floor carries no positive weight and only zeroes the total on violation. Cores cluster at 0.16-0.20 each, secondaries at 0.03-0.07 each. Anti-effort goal_0 is the in-rubric defense against BPE memorization + stdout-string verifier surface; goal_floor is the orthogonal defense against system-level cheats (exfiltration, fabrication) that goal_0's 0.17 weight cannot fully neutralize. Each goal carries an explicit covers_user_asks list mapping it back to indexed user asks in _provenance.user_asks_indexed; coverage is complete (all 10 indexed asks covered by at least one goal). Goal_8 scores the user-dictated Functions Summary table — this is USER spec adherence, not oracle-identifier scoring. Other oracle-only details (line counts, comment density, exact print-format literals, test-string contents, example word choices) remain intentionally NOT scored, per the no-oracle-identifiers bar.

checks.check: every goal traces to a USER ask in _provenance.user_asks_indexed
checks.note: All 8 goals carry covers_user_asks lists with non-empty entries from the indexed asks; oracle-only fields (function names, line counts, comment density, exact print-format literals) are explicitly enumerated in _provenance.user_asks_not_scored with reasons.

checks.check: no oracle-specific identifiers leaked into goal text
checks.note: Goal text avoids the oracle's function names (load_data, get_pair_counts, apply_merge, train_bpe, build_vocab, encode/decode), the oracle's test_strings literals (Emma, Xiomara, Mary-Jane, O'Brien, Z), the oracle's example words (Elizabeth, Jennifer), the oracle's print-format literals ('=== ... ===', 'ratio: <float>x', '[PASS]'), and the 227-line oracle length. Goal_2 mentions encode/decode as concepts, not as required identifier names — phrased 'under any name or as part of any structure'.

checks.check: anti-gaming integrated INSIDE the goals (not a separate hidden filter)
checks.note: goal_0 is a first-class scored goal with explicit zeroing condition for stub/fake behavior. goal_6 is a corpus-realness companion. goal_2 + goal_3 + goal_4 all use format-tolerant phrasing to avoid the canonical-audit-identified false-negative pattern (memorized models passing narrow regexes; correct models failing them).

checks.check: goal_0 present and earns at least 0.10 weight
checks.note: goal_0 carries weight 0.18 — large enough to materially penalize stub submissions without dominating the score.

checks.check: weights differ in distribution from F2P gate weights in tests/test.sh
checks.note: See _weight_audit.weights_differ_from_f2p and differences_summary. v3 introduces 2 goals with no F2P counterpart (goal_0, goal_7), folds 2 F2P gates into 1 goal (goal_1 = syntax+executes), and weights the remaining shared-domain goals at different values.
Top-level _notes explicitly flags BPE memorization + prompt-as-spec risk and explains how the rubric responds (anti-stub goal_0, format-tolerant correctness goals, no oracle-identifier scoring).

_dropped_from_v2.reason: v2's _task_quality_flags block (live-internet flag, unpinned-deps flag) is metadata about the task environment, not part of the rubric. v3 keeps the rubric focused on goal definitions. Task-environment quality flags belong in oracle_audit.json or a separate task_quality.json artifact.

_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 had no goal for the Turn-2 'commit this' user ask. Although it's a workflow step rather than the substantive BPE deliverable, the rubric bar requires that 'every goal traces to USER ask' AND that coverage be complete — so the Turn-2 ask must be scored. Weighted low (0.03) to reflect its workflow-only character.

_self_critique_log.criticism_verbatim: Goal 0 is a task-specific anti-stub with weight 0.18 that only zeroes itself; the rubric lacks a weight 0.0 floor that zeroes the entire score for system-level cheats like git-ops exfiltration or zero-edit patches.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Added goal_floor (tier=anti_effort_floor, weight=0.0) as a system-level kill-switch that zeroes the ENTIRE rubric score on confirmed git-ops exfiltration, empty patches, fetched-not-authored implementations, or hard-coded stdout transcripts. Distinct from goal_0 which remains the task-specific BPE-stub guard scoped to its own weight. _weight_audit now records anti_effort_floor metadata explicitly. Goal_0 weight trimmed 0.18 -> 0.17 to make budget for goal_8.

_self_critique_log.criticism_verbatim: The user explicitly dictated 7 function names and signatures in a 'Functions Summary' table, but the rubric intentionally ignores them (Goal 1: 'specific function names... are not scored'), under-pinning the user's strict spec.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Added goal_8 (tier=secondary, weight=0.07) that scores partial credit for honoring the user's 7-row Functions Summary table (instruction.md L107-114): load_data, get_pair_counts, apply_merge, train_bpe, build_vocab, encode, decode. Partial credit: 5-of-7 matching = pass; 7-of-7 = full credit; <5 = fail. Moved U_EXACT_FUNCTION_NAMES from user_asks_not_scored into user_asks_indexed. Updated goal_1 to clarify it scores only script-execution (not function decomposition) and defers naming to goal_8. Rebalanced weights goal_1 0.20->0.18, goal_2 0.22->0.20, goal_3 0.18->0.16, goal_0 0.18->0.17 (net -0.07) to fund goal_8 (+0.07) and keep sum at 1.00. The SWE-bench Verified narrow-test critique cited in the original _not_scored rationale applies to ORACLE identifier scoring, not USER-dictated identifier scoring — fix corrects that misapplication.

_self_critique_log.criticism_verbatim: Rubric under-pins explicit user output requests. User explicitly asked for [PASS/FAIL] output and progress fields 'pair, frequency, sequence length', but goal_2 currently accepts 'checkmark or X' alternatives. Risk: rubric's over-correction for format tolerance ignores explicit output format requirements the user dictated.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Pinned the two USER-dictated output literals that the prior over-corrected format-tolerant phrasing was incorrectly demoting. (a) goal_2: now REQUIRES the literal bracketed tokens '[PASS]' / '[FAIL]' for per-case round-trip status (instruction.md L93 verbatim 'each shows [PASS/FAIL]' and L135 'All round-trip tests print [PASS]'); explicitly rejects checkmark / X / bare PASS-FAIL alternatives because the user never named them. Test-input contents (test_strings list) remain category-described to preserve anti-memorization on a separate axis. (b) goal_4: now REQUIRES each periodic progress line to include all four user-named fields 'merge index, pair, frequency, sequence length' (instruction.md L69 verbatim); explicitly rejects tqdm-bar-only or final-summary-only renderings, and rejects 'step K/N' progress lines that drop the pair/frequency/seqlen fields. Per-line separator/layout still unscored. (c) Updated _provenance.oracle_identifiers_explicitly_avoided to remove '[PASS]/[FAIL] brackets' and 'merge N/M:' from the avoid list (they were never oracle-only — '[PASS/FAIL]' is user-verbatim and the four progress field names are user-verbatim). Added a new _provenance.user_dictated_outputs_explicitly_pinned block enumerating the three USER-dictated literal outputs the rubric now pins (round-trip marker, progress fields, function names). No weight changes — both edits tighten existing scored goals without rebalancing the budget. Weight sum remains 1.00 across goal_0..goal_8.

_self_critique_log.criticism_verbatim: Two HIGH-severity verifier false-negatives confirmed in trial: (1) gate_roundtrip bash newline bug — FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -c ... || echo 0) captures '0\n0' when grep returns 0 hits, causing '[ "$FAIL_COUNT" -eq 0 ]' to fail with 'integer expression expected'; costs 0.25 reward on correct submissions. (2) gate_compression narrow regex — 'grep -oP ratio:\s*\K[\d.]+(?=x)' requires the literal oracle prefix 'ratio:', but a correct trial prints 'Compression    : 2.01x'; costs 0.20. (3) LOW-severity gate_training drift — regex 'merge\s\+[0-9]\+/' requires the slash N/M fraction, narrower than goal_4's format-tolerant 4-field spec.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Patched harbor_tasks/no-magic-task-ce67d5/tests/test.sh in three places. (a) gate_roundtrip (line ~166): dropped the '|| echo 0' fallthrough that concatenated grep's '0' result with echo's '0\n'; instead strip non-digits via ${VAR//[^0-9]/} and default to 0 via ${VAR:-0} for both PASS_COUNT and FAIL_COUNT. This produces a clean integer regardless of grep's exit code. (b) gate_compression (line ~184): kept the original 'ratio:\s*<float>x' regex as the primary match, added two cascading fallbacks — first a broadened 'compress(ion)? ... <float>x' pattern (catches the trial's 'Compression    : 2.01x'), then a final 'compression_ratio = <float>' style (catches the no-'x'-suffix variant goal_3 admits). Aligns with goal_3's 'any labeled numeric form is acceptable'. (c) gate_training (line ~201): broadened from 'merge\s+[0-9]+/' to '(merge|step)\s+[0-9]+ ... (pair|freq|seq)' — case-insensitive, matches both 'merge N/M: pair=...' AND non-slash variants like 'step 32 pair=...' as long as one of the user-named field tokens (pair/freq/seq) is present on the same line. Aligns gate_training with goal_4's user-named-fields spec. Stripped non-digits from the MERGE_LINES result via the same ${VAR//[^0-9]/} pattern for safety. No rubric goal text or weight changed — these are verifier fixes only, restoring fidelity between test.sh and the rubric. Expected impact on trial rzNf9XF (Opus): reward 0.55 -> ~0.95 (gate_roundtrip 0->1, gate_compression 0->1; all other gates already passing).
